title: Telemetry Record
description: Single record from a telemetry JSONL file (one file per UTC date under framework-memory/telemetry/<YYYY-MM-DD>.jsonl). Phase 4 contract for the records that hooks._telemetry.emit() and hooks._telemetry.emit_many() produce. Distinct from incident records (incidents are one-file-per-failure with ULID correlation; telemetry is high-volume rotating observability without per-record IDs). Phase 4's closed-loop-quality-scorer is the canonical consumer; Phase 10's closed-loop-incident-retrospective-analyst correlates telemetry against incidents.

timestamp.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp at second precision. emit_many() shares one timestamp across the batch; emit() in a loop produces per-record timestamps.

category.description: Short kebab-case identifier — the primary index readers filter on. Common Phase 2/3/4 values: `hook-failure`, `stamp-write`, `tier-violation`, `subagent-start`, `instructions-loaded`, `task-created`, `task-completed`. Open-vocabulary; new emitters add new categories as they ship.

data.description: Event-specific fields. Open-vocabulary — every category defines its own data shape (e.g. `hook-failure` carries `tool` and `exit`; `stamp-write` carries `gate` and `steps`). The schema does not constrain `data` because the vocabulary grows with every new emitter; per-category sub-schemas are a Phase 10 retrospective-analyst concern, not a Phase 4 contract concern.
